title: Golden Syrupy Tunisian Tagine
description: A light and umami-rich tagine dish featuring the sweetness of golden syrup, perfect for a crowd-pleasing meal. Serve warm over couscous or rice.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the bottom of a tagine or large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken thighs, ras el hanout, salt, and black pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the golden syrup, chopped parsley, and 200 grams of couscous to the tagine. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the tagine and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the couscous is tender.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Fluff the couscous with a fork and serve the chicken and sauce over the top.
